All 30 proposal sources now work inside one Publishing workflow.

The full template catalog passed automated, runtime, desktop, and mobile checks against an isolated synthetic database. The earlier browser-harness block is resolved. No branch push, pull request, merge, deployment, live database, or customer data action occurred.

  • The former separate Template Library is integrated into the existing Publishing section.

Actual browser evidence

The verified interface

Verification

What was proven

Catalog integrity

30 unique IDs; counts are 14 Proposal, 8 collateral, 3 Agreement, and 5 SOW.

Every starter loads

Each template produced a title, working-copy name, source pointer, terms boundary, and preserved source identity.

Editing and persistence

All nine narrative fields rendered; rename, reset, save, and reload retained the expected source and copy values.

Responsive safety

Both profiles had no horizontal overflow, console errors, page errors, or failed requests.

  • node --check app.js, server.js, and the template validator passed.
  • node scripts/validate-proposal-template-library.js passed.
  • The standard Control Room browser visual-QA harness passed desktop and mobile captures.
  • The stricter W045 interaction harness passed 42 of 42 checks and exercised all 30 templates twice.
  • The verified base commit is 4e1611e2090f8b272285205da4c181bdebc1eb04; the focused tracked diff remains limited to app.js, index.html, scripts/validate-proposal-template-library.js, and styles.css.
Boundary held

This is verified local work, not a release.

  • The database was temporary, local, synthetic-only, and removed from operation after the run.
  • No production or persistent database, schema source, migration source, authentication, secret, startup configuration, or customer data was changed.
  • No branch was pushed, no pull request was opened, and nothing was merged or deployed.
  • The next pull request will still require exact-diff isolation, then an independent non-builder agent review of its exact SHA before John is asked to authorize merge.
Next controlled step

Authorize W046 only if you want the verified Phase-2 branch published for review.
